zh.Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom: 英國威爾斯卡馬特史爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Rhys ap Hywel Sais於1204年出生在英國威爾斯卡馬特史爾時，他的父親Hywel Felyn ap Gruffudd35歲，母親Sara le Sore21歲。他去世於1251年在英國威爾斯享年47歲。

zh-hans.Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom: 英国威尔斯卡馬特史爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rhys ap Hywel Sais于1204年出生在英国威尔斯卡馬特史爾时，他的父亲Hywel Felyn ap Gruffudd35岁，母亲Sara le Sore21岁。他去世于1251年在英国威尔斯享年47岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hywel
meanings: A Welsh name. Some write the name Hoel, which they render 'lordly;' if so, it may come from huail, a vice-regent, regent. Arthur derives the surname Howell from Corn. -Brit, houl, the sun; and he adds, high, exalted.' But Hywel is rather from hywel, conspicuous.

nameMeanings.name: Sais
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Fernando, Orlando, Angelita, Arturo, Delfina, Desiderio, Dionicio, Edgardo, Elva, Fidel, Isidoro, Jere.
Catalan: topographic name from saix ‘rock’ (from Latin saxum), or an ethnic name for a Saxon, derived from the root of saxó ‘Saxon’.

nameMeanings.name: Rhys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name meaning ‘ardour’; borne in the Middle Ages by various rulers in south-west Wales, including  Rhys ap Tewdur  (  d. 1093 ) and  Rhys ap Gruffudd  (  1132–97 ).

variants.name: Reece
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Rhys . The name is now in widespread and frequent use outside Wales, in many cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the given name.

variants.name: Rees
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Welsh Rhys, in some cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the Welsh given name.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: When Rhys ap Hywel Sais was born in 1204, in Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Hywel Felyn ap Gruffudd, was 35 and his mother, Sara le Sore, was 21. He died in 1251, in Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 47.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rhys ap Hywel Sais est né en 1204, à Carmarthenshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hywel Felyn ap Gruffudd, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Sara le Sore, avait 21 ans. Il est décédé en 1251, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 47 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rhys ap Hywel Sais 1204 in Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hywel Felyn ap Gruffudd 35 und seine Mutter Sara le Sore 21. Er starb 1251 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Wales, United Kingdom.

ht.Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom: Carmarthenshire, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rhys ap Hywel Sais è nato il nel 1204 nel Carmarthenshire, Galles, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Hywel Felyn ap Gruffudd, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Sara le Sore, aveva 21 anni. Morì nel 1251, nel Galles, Regno Unito, all'età di 47 anni.

ja.Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom: イギリスウェールズカーマースエンシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Rhys ap Hywel Saisが1204年にイギリスウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Hywel Felyn ap Gruffuddは35歳，母Sara le Soreは21歳であった。彼は1251年にイギリスウェールズで47歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Rhys ap Hywel Sais(이)가 1204년 영국 웨일스 카마테실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hywel Felyn ap Gruffudd(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Sara le Sore(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1251년 영국 웨일스에서 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

mg.Carmarthenshire, Wales, United Kingdom: Carmarthenshire, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rhys ap Hywel Sais nasceu em 1204, em Carmarthenshire, Gales, United Kingdom, seu pai, Hywel Felyn ap Gruffudd, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Sara le Sore, tinha 21 anos. Ele faleceu em 1251, em Gales, United Kingdom, com 47 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rhys ap Hywel Sais родился в 1204 г. в местности кармартеншир, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Hywel Felyn ap Gruffudd было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Sara le Sore, 21. Он умер в 1251 г. в местности Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 47 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Rhys ap Hywel Sais nació en 1204, en Carmarthenshire, Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Hywel Felyn ap Gruffudd, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Sara le Sore, tenía 21 años. Murió en 1251, en Gales, Reino Unido, a la edad de 47 años.
